MPIPE_EDMA_DESC_t Union Reference
[mPIPE Device API (gxio/mpipe.h)]

MPIPE eDMA Descriptor. More...


Data Fields

 Generation number.
   uint_reg_t   efifo_sel: 6
 For devices with EDMA reorder support, this field allows the descriptor to select the egress FIFO.

 Reserved.

 Checksum generation enabled for this transfer.

 Nothing to be sent.
   uint_reg_t   notif: 1
 Notification interrupt will be delivered when packet has been egressed.

 Destination of checksum relative to CSUM_START relative to the first byte moved by this descriptor.

 Start byte of checksum relative to the first byte moved by this descriptor.
   uint_reg_t   csum_seed: 16
 Initial value for 16-bit 1's compliment checksum if enabled via CSUM.
   int_reg_t   va: 42
 Virtual address.
   uint_reg_t   __reserved_0: 6
 Reserved.
   uint_reg_t   stack_idx: 5
 Index of the buffer stack to which this buffer belongs.

 Encoded size of buffer.
   uint_reg_t   c: 2
 Chaining configuration for the buffer.
}; 
uint_reg_t words [2]
 Word access.

Detailed Description

MPIPE eDMA Descriptor.

The eDMA descriptor is written by software and consumed by hardware. It is used to specify the location of egress packet data to be sent out of the chip via one of the packet interfaces.

Examples:

mpipe/chaining/app.c, mpipe/forward/app.c, mpipe/forward/app_gx8072.c, and mpipe/loopback/app.c.

Field Documentation

uint_reg_t MPIPE_EDMA_DESC_t::gen

Generation number.

Used to indicate a valid descriptor in ring. When a new descriptor is written into the ring, software must toggle this bit. The net effect is that the GEN bit being written into new descriptors toggles each time the ring tail pointer wraps.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_EDMA_DESC_t::efifo_sel

For devices with EDMA reorder support, this field allows the descriptor to select the egress FIFO.

The associated DMA ring must have ALLOW_EFIFO_SEL enabled.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_EDMA_DESC_t::r0

Reserved.

Must be zero.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_EDMA_DESC_t::csum

Checksum generation enabled for this transfer.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_EDMA_DESC_t::ns

Nothing to be sent.

Used, for example, when software has dropped a packet but still wishes to return all of the associated buffers.

Examples:
mpipe/forward/app.c, and mpipe/forward/app_gx8072.c.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_EDMA_DESC_t::notif

Notification interrupt will be delivered when packet has been egressed.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_EDMA_DESC_t::bound

Boundary indicator.

When 1, this transfer includes the EOP for this command. Must be clear on all but the last descriptor for an egress packet.

Examples:
mpipe/chaining/app.c, and mpipe/loopback/app.c.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_EDMA_DESC_t::r1

Reserved.

Must be zero.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_EDMA_DESC_t::xfer_size

Number of bytes to be sent for this descriptor.

When zero, no data will be moved and the buffer descriptor will be ignored. If the buffer descriptor indicates that it is chained, the low 7 bits of the VA indicate the offset within the first buffer (e.g. 127 bytes is the maximum offset into the first buffer). If the size exceeds a single buffer, subsequent buffer descriptors will be fetched prior to processing the next eDMA descriptor in the ring.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_EDMA_DESC_t::r2

Reserved.

Must be zero.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_EDMA_DESC_t::csum_dest

Destination of checksum relative to CSUM_START relative to the first byte moved by this descriptor.

Must be zero if CSUM=0 in this descriptor. Must be less than XFER_SIZE (e.g. the first byte of the CSUM_DEST must be within the span of this descriptor).

uint_reg_t MPIPE_EDMA_DESC_t::csum_start

Start byte of checksum relative to the first byte moved by this descriptor.

If this is not the first descriptor for the egress packet, CSUM_START is still relative to the first byte in this descriptor. Must be zero if CSUM=0 in this descriptor.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_EDMA_DESC_t::csum_seed

Initial value for 16-bit 1's compliment checksum if enabled via CSUM.

Specified in network order. That is, bits[7:0] will be added to the byte pointed to by CSUM_START and bits[15:8] will be added to the byte pointed to by CSUM_START+1 (with appropriate 1's compliment carries). Must be zero if CSUM=0 in this descriptor.

int_reg_t MPIPE_EDMA_DESC_t::va

Virtual address.

Must be sign extended by consumer.

Examples:
mpipe/chaining/app.c.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_EDMA_DESC_t::__reserved_0

Reserved.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_EDMA_DESC_t::stack_idx

Index of the buffer stack to which this buffer belongs.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_EDMA_DESC_t::__reserved_1

Reserved.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_EDMA_DESC_t::inst

Instance ID.

For devices that support automatic buffer return between mPIPE instances, this field indicates the buffer owner. If the INST field does not match the mPIPE's instance number when a packet is egressed, buffers with HWB set will be returned to the other mPIPE instance. Note that not all devices support multi-mPIPE buffer return. The MPIPE_EDMA_INFO.REMOTE_BUFF_RTN_SUPPORT bit indicates whether the INST field in the buffer descriptor is populated by iDMA hardware.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_EDMA_DESC_t::hwb

Always set to one by hardware in iDMA packet descriptors.

For eDMA, indicates whether the buffer will be released to the buffer stack manager. When 0, software is responsible for releasing the buffer.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_EDMA_DESC_t::size

Encoded size of buffer.

Set by the ingress hardware for iDMA packet descriptors. For eDMA descriptors, indicates the buffer size if .c indicates a chained packet. If an eDMA descriptor is not chained and the .hwb bit is not set, this field is ignored and the size is specified by the .xfer_size field. 0 = 128 bytes 1 = 256 bytes 2 = 512 bytes 3 = 1024 bytes 4 = 1664 bytes 5 = 4096 bytes 6 = 10368 bytes 7 = 16384 bytes

uint_reg_t MPIPE_EDMA_DESC_t::c

Chaining configuration for the buffer.

Indicates that an ingress packet or egress command is chained across multiple buffers, with each buffer's size indicated by the .size field.

Examples:
mpipe/chaining/app.c.

uint_reg_t MPIPE_EDMA_DESC_t::words[2]

Word access.

Examples:
mpipe/chaining/app.c.

The documentation for this union was generated from the following file:
  • arch/mpipe_shm.h
